title: Executive Order 9621—Termination of the Office of Strategic Services and Disposition of Its Functions

flags.justification: The Secretaries of State and War have wind-down discretion over an explicitly temporary interim body, not ongoing operational concentration.
A clear chain of responsibility with a specific, dated termination milestone (December 31, 1945) for the interim Service.
Splitting OSS functions between State (intelligence/research) and War (remaining functions) is one coherent reorganization action.
An internal government reorganization, not a third-party compliance regime.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
The Interim Research and Intelligence Service is explicitly 'abolished as of the close of business December 31, 1945' (Sec. 2) — a clean, explicit termination date.
An internal reorganization with no third-party compliance regime.

The order's text is narrowly scoped and explicitly self-terminating; its later historical role as the institutional precursor to the CIA came through separate legislative action two years afterward, not through authority claimed in this text.
OSS functions are carefully, explicitly divided between State and War Departments with specific criteria for each.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. The Interim Research and Intelligence Service created here at State became the institutional seed of the CIA's analytical function, but this order's own text is narrowly and explicitly self-terminating — consistent with the methodology's principle of scoring the text, not later historical significance.
